fact: November 11 is exactly 315 days into the year - that's 45 weeks of pure calendar magic!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely bonkers burying nuts everywhere, forgetting where half of them are hidden!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern places on November 11, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
November 11 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under black light!
In many forests, this is peak leaf-crunching season when stepping on dried leaves sounds like walking on nature's bubble wrap.
Deer are in their winter coat-growing phase around November 11, making them look extra fluffy and ready for cold snaps.
The date sits exactly 41 days before winter solstice in the north, meaning daylight is shrinking by about 2-3 minutes every single day.
In Australia and other southern places, November 11 brings the smell of eucalyptus blooms as spring flowers explode into action.
This date marks when many northern lakes start forming their first thin sheets of ice that crack and pop like Rice Krispies.
November 11 is when you can see your breath in the morning air in most cold places - like having a personal fog machine built into your lungs!
Geese flying south create their famous V-formations around this time, honking so loudly they sound like flying car horns.
The moon phase on November 11 changes every year, but when it's full around this date, it's called the Beaver Moon - named after busy dam-building season!
